about.title: About the Project
about.description: Discover the research objectives, datasets, machine learning models, and system architecture of the Student Early Warning System.

overview.title: Project Overview
overview.p1: The AI-powered Student Early Warning System (SEWS) is developed to address the critical challenge of academic dropout in higher education. By analyzing demographic, socio-economic, admission, and semester-level academic performance logs, the system calculates an individual Risk Score for each student. This allows academic advisors and faculty counseling departments to step in with personalized interventions before a student drops out.
overview.p2: Rather than a simple predictions machine, the system is designed to provide Explainable AI (XAI) metrics, pinpointing why a student is flagged as high-risk, enabling institutions to deploy precise financial, academic, or counseling support.
overview.obj1: Early identification of student attrition risks.
overview.obj4: Decoupled API integration for scalable school deploys.

dataset.title: Dataset Profile: UCI Student Attrition
dataset.p1: The SEWS AI engine utilizes the 'Predict Students' Dropout and Academic Success' dataset, sourced from the UCI Machine Learning Repository (ID: 697). It consists of administrative records collected from a higher education institution in Portugal. It contains 4,424 records with 36 core academic and demographic features.

algorithms.title: Model Training Algorithms (Ensemble Classifiers)
algorithms.rfDesc: Constructs a multitude of decision trees at training time and outputs the mode/mean classification of the individual trees. Highly stable and prevents model overfitting.
algorithms.xgbDesc: An optimized distributed gradient boosting library designed to be highly efficient, flexible, and portable. Excellent at learning complex boundaries in tabular data.
algorithms.lgbmDesc: Developed by Microsoft, it uses tree-based learning algorithms. Fast training speeds, low memory usage, and handles large datasets with categorical features natively.

stack.title: Decoupled System Tech Stack
stack.clientDesc: Vue 3, Pinia, Tailwind CSS v4, PrimeVue v4, TypeScript.
stack.gatewayNote: No backend API calls are implemented in this phase. The frontend uses a local DB to simulate all operations.

desc.vue: Progressive JavaScript framework for UI components
desc.pinia: Reactive state management pattern and store
desc.tailwind: Utility-first CSS framework for modern design aesthetics
desc.primevue: Rich component library styled with Aura design presets
desc.typescript: Strong static typings for enterprise codebase consistency
desc.laravel: PHP framework for administrative API & role control (Future)
desc.postgresql: Relational database for secure history and profile logs (Future)
desc.fastapi: Python API framework for high-throughput AI predictions
desc.xgb_lgbm: Gradient boosted tree algorithms achieving 87%+ PR-AUC

team.title: Research & Development Team
team.initiative: Vietnam National University - Ho Chi Minh City (VNU-HCM) / Early Warning Research Initiative

Graduate: Continue maintaining current excellent academic performance and attendance.
Consider applying to act as a peer mentor or tutor for junior students.
Explore undergraduate research opportunities or honors projects.
Participate in career counseling and upcoming internships in your faculty.
Enrolled: Schedule a meeting with your academic advisor to review credit progression.
Utilize university library study rooms and resources for course evaluations.
Participate in subject-specific review workshops before mid-semester assessments.
Confirm all course enrollment details and prerequisites for upcoming units.
Dropout: Schedule an urgent guidance session with the faculty student support services.
Contact the financial affairs department to review outstanding fees and installment options.
Participate in peer-tutoring programs for modules with low success rates.
Evaluate options for transitioning to evening class attendance to manage daytime commitments.
Consider adjusting credit enrollment load to balance academic and personal requirements.
